query: What detailed fees are associated with each decision point in the NYISO generation interconnection process?
final_answer:
Below are the **fees/deposits that attach to the main “go / no‑go” decision points** in the **NYISO generation interconnection process**, with the **best-available tariff / NYISO-source amounts**. Where NYISO charges are *not a fixed number*, NYISO typically collects a **deposit and then “trues up” to actual study costs on a time-and-materials basis** (i.e., you pay actual costs; unused deposit is refunded; if costs exceed deposit you pay the balance).

---

## A) “Legacy” NYISO LFIP / Class Year framework (Attachment X – Large Facilities >20 MW)

### 1) Submit an Interconnection Request (enter the queue)
**Decision point:** file vs. don’t file.  
**Fees due at filing:**
- **$10,000 non‑refundable application fee** (split **50/50 between NYISO and the Connecting Transmission Owner(s)**).  
- **$30,000 refundable study deposit** for the **Interconnection Feasibility Study**.  

Source (NYISO OATT Section 30.3.1 / LFIP excerpt): NYISO tariff text shows the **$10,000** non-refundable fee and **$30,000** refundable feasibility deposit required with the Interconnection Request.  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1404025/OATT%252030-3%2520-%2520BSM%2520Changes_Repowering%2520_ICAPWG_021914.pdf

---

### 2) Provide Site Control (or post deposit in lieu of Site Control)
**Decision point:** demonstrate site control now vs. later (and risk losing deposit).  
**If you do not provide site control with the Interconnection Request:**
- **$10,000 deposit in lieu of site control** (the referenced tariff appendix describes it as **non‑refundable unless you cure within the allowed period**; if you timely demonstrate site control, it becomes refundable; otherwise it becomes non‑refundable).

Source (LFIP Interconnection Request appendix / instructions):  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/8372822/OATT%2030.14%20+%20CRIS%20Expiration.pdf/78bcbb21-83e8-3b47-7e16-4d36899f995d

---

### 3) Optional Interconnection Feasibility Study (OFES) “depth” election
**Decision point:** limited screening vs. more detailed early work (helps de-risk, but higher deposit).  
From the LFIP appendices / “Terms and Conditions of Interconnection Study(ies)”:
- **$10,000 study deposit** for a **limited analysis** Optional Feasibility Study; **or**
- **$60,000 study deposit** for **more detailed analyses** Optional Feasibility Study.

Source (same NYISO LFIP appendices PDF, showing the $10k vs $60k OFES deposits):  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/8372822/OATT%2030.14%20+%20CRIS%20Expiration.pdf/78bcbb21-83e8-3b47-7e16-4d36899f995d

---

### 4) Study-stage “continue / withdraw” decision points (Feasibility → SRIS → Class Year Facilities Study)
**Decision point:** after each study result, proceed vs. withdraw.  
**Cost responsibility / fees:**
- NYISO/TO generally bills **actual study costs**; deposits are **credited** and then **trued up** (pay additional if deposit is short; refund if deposit exceeds actual costs).
- If you **withdraw**, you typically remain liable for **costs incurred to date** (and any tariff-defined non-refundable portions triggered by late withdrawal / failure to meet requirements).

**Important limitation:** In the sources retrievable here, the **fixed-dollar deposits are clearly stated for:**
- Interconnection Request fee ($10k) and Feasibility Study deposit ($30k), and
- Optional Feasibility Study deposits ($10k limited / $60k detailed).

However, the **specific fixed deposit amounts for SRIS and Class Year Facilities Study** (if any fixed values apply under the exact tariff version you mean) are referenced as being detailed in NYISO materials (e.g., TEI Manual tables / Attachment X sections), but those tables were **not accessible in full text here** to quote the exact numbers reliably. Practically, developers should expect **additional deposits and full cost responsibility** for:
- **SRIS (System Reliability Impact Study)** costs, and
- **Class Year Interconnection Facilities Study** costs,
both subject to **actual-cost true-up** and then **acceptance of upgrade cost allocations** (CTOAF / SUF / SDU, etc.) if continuing to interconnection agreements.

(If you need, the exact deposits can be pulled directly from the controlling effective version of Attachment X / TEI Manual “Deposits Associated with the NYISO LFIP” table—but I’m not going to guess the numbers without the exact tariff text in hand.)

---

## B) NYISO Order No. 2023 “Cluster Study” process (transitioning / new process)
NYISO’s publicly-posted Order 2023 compliance materials show **new, more standardized deposits** tied to project size and specific decision windows.

### 1) Pre‑Application (optional informational screen)
**Decision point:** do a pre-app screen before entering the cluster.  
- **$5,000 per Point of Interconnection (POI)** (per NYISO compliance plan materials).

Source (NYISO Order 2023 compliance plan deck excerpt):  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07_Order%2520No.%25202023%2520Compliance%2520Plan_OC_20231214_Final.pdf

---

### 2) Cluster Application Window – submit Interconnection Request into the cluster
**Decision point:** enter the cluster (and commit deposits) vs. wait/exit.  
As presented by NYISO:
- **$10,000 application fee** (**$5,000 for CRIS-only projects**)  
- **Study Deposit (examples shown by NYISO):**
  - **< 80 MW:** **$100,000**
  - **≥ 80 MW and < 200 MW:** **$150,000**
  - **≥ 200 MW:** **$250,000**
  - **CRIS-only:** **$50,000**

Source (NYISO Order 2023 compliance plan materials showing study deposit amounts by size):  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43295731/02%20Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan%20and%20Tariff%20Revisions_IITF_20240301.pdf/

---

### 3) Post-study decision windows (iterative decisions / withdrawal penalties / readiness deposits)
**Decision point:** proceed after results vs. withdraw (and potentially forfeit part of deposits / incur penalties).  
NYISO’s compliance materials outline:
- **Readiness Deposits (RD1 / RD2) and/or Security postings** keyed to MW and/or % of upgrade cost estimates, and
- **Withdrawal penalties** (e.g., percentage of study deposit at certain stages, escalating later).

Source (same March 1, 2024 IITF deck):  
https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43295731/02%20Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan%20and%20Tariff%20Revisions_IITF_20240301.pdf/

---

## What “fees at each decision point” usually means in practice (NYISO)
Even where a step doesn’t have a fixed “fee,” there is still a financial decision point:
- **Enter / stay in process:** pay **application fee (non-refundable)** + post **study deposits**.
- **Choose study depth:** higher optional study deposits can reduce later uncertainty.
- **After each study:** decide whether to **accept results and continue** (often requiring **additional deposits / security** and ultimately paying **CTOAF/SUF/SDU** upgrade allocations) or **withdraw** (paying incurred costs; possibly losing non-refundable amounts or paying withdrawal penalties under the applicable ruleset).

---

### Primary NYISO sources used (for audit trail)
- LFIP Section 30.3.1 excerpt (Interconnection Request fee + feasibility deposit):  
  https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1404025/OATT%252030-3%2520-%2520BSM%2520Changes_Repowering%2520_ICAPWG_021914.pdf  
- LFIP Appendix / Interconnection Request form and study terms (site control deposit; OFES deposits $10k/$60k):  
  https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/8372822/OATT%2030.14%20+%20CRIS%20Expiration.pdf/78bcbb21-83e8-3b47-7e16-4d36899f995d  
- Order 2023 compliance materials (pre-app fee; cluster study deposits by size; application fees; readiness/withdrawal constructs):  
  https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07_Order%2520No.%25202023%2520Compliance%2520Plan_OC_20231214_Final.pdf  
  https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43295731/02%20Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan%20and%20Tariff%20Revisions_IITF_20240301.pdf/

---

If you need this reformatted as a **single end-to-end “decision tree” with $ amounts on every branch**, the only missing piece is confirming the **exact tariff version** you mean for the **legacy SRIS + Class Year Facilities Study deposit schedule** (those values can vary by tariff revision). Without that, I can’t responsibly fill in those two rows with numbers.
